question: There are 80 boys and girls in a hall. After 1/4 of the boys and 5 girls left, the number of boys became equal to the number of girls. How many boys were there at first?
The ratio of boys to girls in a class was 3:2. After 10 boys and 5 girls joined the class, the ratio became 4:3. How many pupils were in the class at first?
There were 360 apples and oranges in a basket. 25% of the apples were eaten and 20 oranges were added. In the end, there were equal numbers of apples and oranges. How many apples were there at first?
The number of boys is 3 times the number of girls. After 20 boys and 12 girls left, the number of boys became 4 times the number of girls. How many girls were there at first?
There were 480 stamps in two albums. Album A lost 20% of its stamps. Album B gained 30 stamps. In the end, both albums had the same number of stamps. How many stamps were in Album A at first?
The ratio of Adam's money to Ben's money was 5:3. After Adam spent 40% of his money and Ben received $20, they had the same amount. How much money did Adam have at first?

There are 280 students in Hall A and Hall B. 1/4 of the students in Hall A moved to Hall B. Then 15 students left Hall B. In the end, both halls had the same number of students. How many students were in Hall A at first?

The number of fiction books is 4 times the number of non-fiction books. After 50 fiction books were borrowed and 10 non-fiction books were added, the number of fiction books became 3 times the number of non-fiction books. How many non-fiction books were there at first?
There were 420 cows and goats on a farm. 30% of the cows were sold and 25 goats were bought. In the end, there were equal numbers of cows and goats. How many cows were there at first?
The ratio of red marbles to blue marbles was 7:4. After 21 red marbles were removed and 12 blue marbles were added, the ratio became 1:1. How many red marbles were there at first?

There were 500 beads in Box X and Box Y. 1/5 of the beads in Box X were moved to Box Y. Then 20 beads were removed from Box Y. In the end, both boxes had the same number of beads. How many beads were in Box X at first?
The number of boys is 2 times the number of girls. After 15 boys left and 9 girls joined, the number of boys became equal to the number of girls. How many pupils were there at first?

There were 380 pens and pencils in a drawer. 35% of the pens were removed and 14 pencils were removed. In the end, the number of pens and pencils remaining was equal. How many pens were there at first?
The ratio of Tom's stamps to Jerry's stamps was 9:5. After Tom gave 28 stamps to Jerry, the ratio became 1:1. How many stamps did Tom have at first?
There are 320 students in a school. 40% are boys. After some boys joined the school, the percentage of boys became 50%. How many boys joined the school?

The number of apples is 5 times the number of oranges. After 40 apples were eaten and 8 oranges were eaten, the number of apples became 4 times the number of oranges. How many oranges were there at first?

There were 450 marbles in Jar P and Jar Q. 20% of the marbles in Jar P were moved to Jar Q. Then 30 marbles were removed from Jar Q. In the end, both jars had the same number of marbles. How many marbles were in Jar P at first?
The ratio of Sarah's money to Jane's money was 8:5. After Sarah spent $30 and Jane received $15, they had the same amount. How much money did Sarah have at first?

There were 280 fiction and non-fiction books in a library. 1/7 of the fiction books and 20 non-fiction books were removed. In the end, there were equal numbers of both types. How many fiction books were there at first?
The number of boys is 3 times the number of girls. After 24 boys left and 8 girls joined, the number of boys became 2 times the number of girls. How many girls were there at first?
